entry.title: Vector Reveals Upcoming Boeing 787 Dreamliner for MSFS24
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Brand-new development studio “Vector” has recently unveiled their very first project, the Boeing 787 Dreamliner, intended to be released for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024. ‍ Vector is composed of experienced developers from the flight sim community, coming together to make what they aim to be “the best aircraft in flight simulation”. The new studio becomes another player in a growing market for Boeing airliners for the Microsoft Flight Simulator platform, joining the likes of PMDG, iFly, and Bluebird Simulations. ‍ Vector’s rendition of the Boeing 787 Dreamliner, codenamed “Project Griffin”, has been developed specifically for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, in collaboration with active Boeing 787 pilots. The team says that Project Griffin is “built for serious simulation enthusiasts”, and that their philosophy “[brings] together visuals, systems, and workflow into a cohesive experience”. Exact features and system depth have not been announced, but the team says that this will be announced “progressively as the project advances”. ‍ Vector is targeting a release date sometime this year, but more specifics have not been shared. ‍ Threshold will keep you updated on all the news regarding this exciting new product, so stay tuned! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: X-Crafts Releases ERJ Family 2.0 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. X-Crafts has released its new ERJ Family 2.0 for X-Plane 12, a rebuilt-from-scratch, new generation of its Embraer regional jets, covering the ERJ 135, ERJ 140, ERJ 145, ERJ 145XR, and the Legacy 650. The developer says the focus this time is a full, “real-world ops” systems simulation across the range, pairing a pilot-approved flight model with an authentic FMS and autopilot that supports procedures like holds, flyover waypoints, DME arcs, and intercepts. A major new addition is AirComms, an on-screen guidance system designed to keep you moving without turning the cockpit into a checklist marathon, offering reminders, warnings, and step-by-step prompts from startup to shutdown, with the option to disable it once you are comfortable. Version 2.0 also introduces a new avionics suite, with high-resolution PFD, MFD, and EICAS displays, synoptic pages, a custom navigation map, weather radar, Terrain Radar compatibility, and resizable pop-out displays. The ERJ Family 2.0 also includes a fully integrated EFB, covering doors and ground services, weight and balance with SimBrief integration, take-off and landing calculations, interactive checklists, and aircraft settings, alongside AviTab integration. X-Crafts notes that the passenger ERJs do not feature VNAV or autothrottle in real-world service, and these functions are not included here. Although some Legacy aircraft do have them, they are not currently simulated. Visually, the aircraft features a retextured interior, updated gear modelling with optional clean or dirty textures, new lighting, rain effects, VR-ready cockpit improvements, and performance optimisation via LOD tuning. X-Crafts’ ERJ Family costs USD 139.95 as a bundle, or individually at USD 69.95 (Legacy 650, E145/E145XR) and USD 59.95 (E135, E140), with an upgrade to the remaining four variants for USD 79.99. Full details are on the X-Plane.org Store product page, or click here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Orbx Releases McKinnon St. Simons Island Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Orbx has recently released their rendition of McKinnon St. Simons Island Airport (KSSI) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving Brunswick, Georgia, with an average of 43,070 aircraft operations per year. It opened in early 1938, after three years of construction work. Its infrastructure and strategic location were made useful by the US Navy, becoming Naval Air Station St. Simons Island. It remained an active air station for a few years after the war, before its activities were merged into NAS Glynco, ultimately leading to its closure as a naval air station and its return as a civil airport. Back in the day, the airport saw some commercial activity from Delta (1945-1960s) and Eastern Airlines (1945-1964), but it has since become a general aviation airport. The scenery features an accurate rendition of the airport, with custom ground textures, ultra-high definition PBR texturing, seamless photogrammetry integration, custom night lighting, photorealistic HD textures, and more. It’s available on OrbxDirect for roughly $19.18, requiring at least 2.99 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Drzewiecki Design Releases KEWR for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Renowned developer Drzewiecki Design has released their highly anticipated Newark Liberty International Airport (KEWR) scenery for X-Plane 12. KEWR serves as a major hub for United Airlines, as well as hosting significant operations for other major airlines in the USA, including JetBlue, Spirit Airlines, and American Airlines. Having first opened in 1928, the airport now serves as a major transatlantic hub, with frequent flights to Europe, the Middle East, and certain flights to Asia and South America. The airport serves as a key domestic gateway, featuring flights to cities such as San Francisco, Orlando, and Los Angeles. Lastly, KEWR sees significant cargo operations, which cements its spot as both a major passenger destination, but also an essential cargo hub, offering a variety of flights to do in the simulator. KEWR contains numerous features such as full PBR texturing, night lighting, weather effects, and more. The full feature list can be viewed below. If you wish to purchase KEWR for X-Plane 12, you can do so on the X-Plane.org Store, for a price of $24 (USD).

entry.title: iniBuilds Release VHHH Trailer
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Developer iniBuilds have released the trailer and additional information for their much anticipated rendition of Hong Kong International Airport and it's shaping up to be a stunner. The development group took to their forums to announce the project is in the final development stages and it should be an on time arrival in Hong Kong for fans. iniBuilds have disclosed that “The complexity of its (VHHH) layout, combined with its proximity to one of the most recognisable skylines in the world, has made this an incredibly satisfying project to work on” While also disclosing that an on-site visit was carried out by the team giving them the opportunity to gather plenty of photographic and video references to use in creating an accurate rendition of the airport. Scenery features according to the developer include: A footnote added that the currently under construction terminal will be added free of charge in a future update. Now, what would VHHH be without the stunning metropolis of Hong Kong and its awe-inspiring skyline? Well no need to worry, inibuilds have disclosed that a Hong Kong city POI pack will also be released free of charge to those who purchase the scenery! You can watch the trailer here: Threshold will be sure to keep you updated as this progresses.

entry.title: FlightFX HJet HA-420 Released for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FlightFX has released the HJet HA-420 for MSFS 2024, bringing Marwan Gharib’s popular microjet into the new sim, also with a broad rebuild. According to FlightFX, the MSFS 2024 edition focuses on bigger changes under the hood, including a rebuilt flight model, updated ground handling and suspension physics, and an avionics backend moved to the Working Title V2 framework, with a custom Vertical Situation Display (VSD) to match. On the visuals side, the package utilises MSFS 2024-native materials and shaders, a remodelled cockpit, and a refreshed cabin with interactive elements, smart glass-style window shades, and the now-not-so-secret cabin companion, Rufus. Audio has also been redone, with FlightFX listing a new soundscape by Echo 19, alongside updated engine, ground roll, and cockpit ambience work. FlightFX is also shipping the HJet with its VAMS 2.0 EFB, including Navigraph integration, SimBrief-supported weight and balance tools, and additional utilities, plus native support for MSFS 2024 walk-around interactions. The developer also highlights compatibility with MSFS 2024 Career Mode. Flight Physics Avionics and Systems Visual Modeling Audio Electronic Flight Bag (VAMS 2.0) Miscellaneous FlightFX’s release post lists the HJet at $24.99 USD, with availability across the MSFS 2024 Marketplace, Contrail, the Flightsim.to Store, and Orbx Direct. If you already own the MSFS 2020 HJet, you are entitled to a free upgrade, but the process varies with each vendor. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Annotations and Scratchpads Released for Navigraph Charts
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Navigraph has released both annotations and scratchpads to their charts application, marking a significant quality of life upgrade. With annotations, users can now draw directly on Jeppesen charts and SimBrief operational flight plans. The application supports freehand drawings, as well as simple shapes, to help in highlighting essential information on charts for your flights. Annotations ‍ The scratchpad works completely within the app as well, allowing users to copy clearances, holding instructions, ATIS data, pireps, and more. This is super useful as you do not need to switch apps or use paper to write instructions now, as it can all be done within the app. Scratchpad ‍ The update is out now to all Navigraph Unlimited subscribers. To view more information, check out the original post on the Navigraph website.

entry.title: FS2Crew Updates Animated PMDG 737 First Officer Bringing MSFS 2024 Support
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FS2Crew has recently released significant updates for their animated First Officer for the PMDG 737 series, with the main highlight being full support for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024. FS2Crew’s Animated First Officer features a fully modelled first officer who, according to the developer, performs flows “just as in the real world.” The ability to choose between multiple models for the first officer is also present. All three Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) packs are receiving this update, which includes compatibility for the PMDG 737-600, 737-800, and 737-900 variants. Existing customers can update their packs free of charge through the FS2Crew Product Manager. FS2Crew also states that many of their other products have received updates, enhancing compatibility, stability, and the user experience. For more on FS2Crew’s animated First Officer, check out FS2Crew’s website. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: VFRScenery Announces Walkaround V2 for X-Plane
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. VFRScenery, the developer of the Walkaround Plugin for X-Plane 11 and 12, has announced that an updated version will be released on March 7th. Walkaround V2 aims to deliver a comprehensive walkaround experience for both X-Plane 11 and 12. This new version features an immersive audio system, a fully redesigned user interface, and enhanced customisation options for both general aviation aircraft and airliners. One of the main highlights is the complete integration of the "WalkCabin mode," which allows users to perform an exterior check, climb stairs, and seamlessly transition into the cabin. Additional features include an advanced camera system that simulates realistic pilot height, as well as binoculars and a flashlight. There is also a landing rate module that provides commentary from the first officer and displays statistics such as feet per minute (FPM), G-Force, landing speed, and a calculated score. Walkaround V2 is releasing exclusively on the x-plane.org store on the seventh of March. The update is offered free of charge to owners of the previous version. ‍ For more about the Walkaround V2 Plugin, check out VFRScenery’s YouTube video. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: CSS Provide Blog Update on 737 Classic V2
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Development group CSS Simulations early this year provided an in depth look at their upcoming 737 Classic V2 update which will further enhance the aircraft. The development of V2 is built around improving visual and systems quality along with bringing new features and further refinements. CSS have stated that the V2 build is designed around community feedback and they are actively engaging with the community to provide a fantastic rendition of the “Classic” In regards to the interior they have stated: The exterior is also set to receive a big overhaul including enhancements: They have also stated that the -300 and -400 variants are almost ready to be released in their V1 configuration. Threshold will keep you updated as we learn more. ‍

entry.title: MK Studios Updates Düsseldorf Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has recently issued an update to their rendition of Düsseldorf Airport (EDDL) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020/2024, bringing it up to date with real-life layout changes and more. Update 1.1.0 ensures the ground layout matches Düsseldorf Airport as it is in 2026, fixes certain object placements, improves runway markings, and other small fixes. The complete changelog follows below: ​ Changelog 1.1.0 • Ground layout updated according to latest 2026 changes • Object placement fixes • Improved runway marking textures • Various small fixes To celebrate the update, the scenery will be on sale (40% off) until Tuesday (10/03). It’s available on Contrail for roughly $12.50 (promotional price), requiring at least 4.4 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: DominicDesignTeam Releases Indianapolis International Airport for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. DominicDesignTeam have released their rendition of Indianapolis International Airport (KIND) for X-Plane 12. Located just 7 miles from downtown Indianapolis, it is the largest airport in Indiana, serving the city of Indianapolis, with over 10 million passenger operations in the last year. Opened in 1931, it replaced Stout Field as the main airport serving Indianapolis. In 1944, it was renamed to Weir Cook Municipal Airport in honour of Harvey Weir Cook, a World War I flying ace. The airport was also one of the stops on the first transcontinental air service between Los Angeles and New York, operated by Transcontinental Air Transport, which later became the iconic TWA. In 1961, the first jet aircraft to touch down at KIND was a TWA Convair 880, which was later followed by a Boeing B707. During the late 1980s, the airport also became a hub for USAir (which later became US Airways), ATA Airways, and FedEx, which made Indianapolis their second-largest hub, after Memphis. Today, Indianapolis Intl’ Airport is served by 14 passenger airlines to over 50 destinations, including 6 international destinations, with five of them being seasonal service to popular tourist destinations in Mexico, Puerto Rico and the Dominican Republic, and by 5 cargo airlines, to over 60 destinations, mostly in USA. The DominicDesignTeam’s rendition of KIND features custom made terminal, FBO and surrounding buildings, custom mesh and ground clutter, taxi signs, 3D grass, dynamic lighting, as well as support for WorldTraffic 3 and Traffic Global routes. The scenery is available for purchase on the X-Plane.org store for $19.99, and requires at least 1.4GB of free disk space. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Releases Hong Kong International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently released their rendition of Hong Kong International Airport (VHHH) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, the 8th busiest airport in the World, with an average of 71 million passengers per year. Hong Kong International was built to replace the old Kai Tak Airport, deemed too small to handle the traffic it was handling, and to address the insane number of complaints from airlines due to the delays, a direct consequence of the fact that it could not scale up to the demand. Planning studies started in 1974, with construction work only kicking in almost 20 years later, in 1991. Completion was achieved in early 1998. Built on a large artificial island, the total area of the airport (12.48 square kilometers) corresponds to nearly 1% of Hong Kong’s total area. The airport is a hub for Cathay Pacific, Greater Bay Express, HK Express, and Hong Kong Airlines, and is visited by over 100 other airlines with more than 180 destinations. It’s also the World’s busiest cargo airport, hosting Air Hong Kong, Cathay Cargo, DHL Aviation, Hong Kong Airlines Cargo, Kalitta Air, Polar Air Cargo, and UPS Airlines. The scenery features an up-to-date ground layout, true-to-life landside, handcrafted ground textures, custom jetways, highly detailed terminals, performance-optimized performance, custom night lighting, and a Point of Interest pack for Hong Kong landmarks. It’s available on the iniBuilds Store for roughly $26.72, requiring at least 6.27 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ​ ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates A300-600R Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently issued an update to their A300-600R Premium Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, improving systems, performance, visuals, sounds, and overall usability. Update v1.0.7 delivers significant performance gains across a wide range of systems, from low-end to high-end. On their low-spec test system with a mobile RTX 4070, the passenger version went from 26.8 FPS to 46.8 FPS, and the freighter went from 30.7 FPS to 43.6 FPS, with stability improvements. On their high-end test system with a 9800X3D and an RTX 4090, the frames per second on the passenger version went from 64.7 FPS to 80.8 FPS, and on the freighter version from 75.7 FPS to 84.3 FPS. They have also implemented numerous autopilot and flight mode fixes, added pause at top of descent, time compression, CPDLC compatibility for BATC/SI, improved EFB startup options, and resolved a variety of art, LOD, and sound issues. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ The A300-600R Premium is available on their store for roughly $60, requiring at least 3.5 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Shares New A340-600 Screenshots for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has shared new screenshots of their A340-600 Pro for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020/2024, developed in conjunction with ToLiss, a renowned X-Plane add-on developer with a vast lineup of high-fidelity Airbus aircraft. Formerly the world’s longest commercial aircraft (up until the arrival of the Boeing 747-800), the A340-600 was designed to compete with early jumbo jets that were getting phased out at the time due to their age, offering the same four engines and similar passenger carrying capabilities, but boasting full fly-by-wire technology. While the range was nothing short of impressive, and the flying experience was smooth and comfortable, the timing was far from perfect, since the early 2000s also brought a beefy long-haul widebody with two engines, the 777-300ER, which was fairly cheaper to operate and ended up being the airplane of choice for many operators around the globe. Aerosoft says things are “moving quickly” with the project and that the upcoming week will be very exciting for everyone waiting for the product. The riddle they teased recently will be solved, and they can’t wait to share what they have been working on. Furthermore, they shared a fresh batch of screenshots that show a bit more of the A340-600's interior and exterior. There’s still no information about the pricing or the eventual release date, but Threshold will keep you updated! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: 29Palms Releases Chania International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. 29Palms has recently released their rendition of Chania International Airport (LGSA) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving the city of Chania, Crete, with an average of 4 million passengers per year. The airport was originally a military base, and commercial flights were transferred from the previous airport (Maleme) due to insufficient capacity in the late 1950s, which eventually plagued the new airport as well, leading to the construction of a new terminal building in the 1990s. The new terminal was inaugurated in 1996, with a surface area of 14,650 square meters and six aircraft stands, designed to handle up to 1.35 million passengers per year. The airport was privatized in 2015 and is now operated by Fraport AG and the Copelouzos Group for the next 40 years, as of April 2017. In mid-2018, the airport saw its first post-privatization upgrades, including an expansion of the passenger safety area, ground layout changes, twice as many hand baggage scanners, new boarding gates, and more. It’s a focus city for Aegean Airlines and Ryanair. Air Serbia, Animawings, Austrian Airlines, British Airways, Brussels Airlines, Condor, Discover Airlines, easyJet, Edelweiss, Eurowings, Finnair, Jet2.com, Luxair, Neos, Norwegian Air Shuttle, Scandinavian Airlines, Sky Express, Smartwings, Transavia, TUI Airways, TUI fly Belgium, TUI fly Netherlands, and Wizz Air also serve it. The scenery features an accurate rendition of the airport, with custom ground textures, highly detailed 3d objects, terminal interiors, sloped runway profile, custom animated wingsocks, and much more. It’s available on Contrail for roughly $18.58, requiring at least 2.32 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍
